title: Add support for multiple date format
description:
Initial analysis by Claude


Supported Date Formats

Based on the parsing logic in TypeConversion.cs:132-156:

Format Category
Format Pattern
Example
Status
Notes
ISO 8601 (Recommended)
yyyy-MM-dd
2024-01-15
✅ Tested
Best choice - unambiguous


yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ
2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
✅ Tested
UTC timezone indicator


yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ
2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z
✅ Tested
With milliseconds


yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
2024-01-15 10:30:00
✅ Tested
Space separator
ISO with Timezone Offset
yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fff+HH:mm
2025-10-04T00:00:00.000+08:00
✅ Tested
Positive offset


yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fff-HH:mm
2025-01-01T00:00:00.000-05:00
✅ Tested
Negative offset
Slash-Separated
yyyy/MM/dd
2025/10/11
✅ Tested
Used by Branch API


MM/dd/yyyy
01/15/2024
✅ Supported
US format only
Month Names (Abbreviated)
MMM dd, yyyy
Jan 15, 2024
✅ Supported
US style with comma


dd MMM yyyy
15 Jan 2024
✅ Supported
EU style


MMM dd yyyy
Jan 15 2024
✅ Supported
No comma


dd-MMM-yyyy
15-Jan-2024
✅ Supported
Dash separator
Month Names (Full)
MMMM dd, yyyy
January 15, 2024
✅ Supported
US style with comma


dd MMMM yyyy
15 January 2024
✅ Supported
EU style


MMMM dd yyyy
January 15 2024
✅ Supported
No comma
DateTime Combinations
yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm
2024-01-15 10:30
✅ Supported
No seconds


yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff
2024-01-15 10:30:00.123
✅ Supported
With milliseconds


MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss
01/15/2024 10:30:00
✅ Supported
US format with time



Unsupported / Problematic Formats

Format Category
Format Pattern
Example
Status
Issue
EU Format (Problematic)
dd/MM/yyyy
15/01/2024
⚠️ FAILS
Day > 12: Parse fails entirely


dd/MM/yyyy
12/01/2024
❌ WRONG
Parsed as Jan 12 instead of Dec 1


dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss
15/01/2024 10:30:00
⚠️ FAILS
Same issue as above
Other Separators
dd.MM.yyyy
15.01.2024
❌ Unsupported
Dot separator not recognized


dd-MM-yyyy
15-01-2024
❌ Unsupported
Ambiguous - might fail
Reversed Formats
yyyy/dd/MM
2024/15/01
❌ Unsupported
Non-standard format
Two-Digit Year
MM/dd/yy
01/15/24
⚠️ Risky
Year interpretation unclear (1924 vs 2024)


dd/MM/yy
15/01/24
❌ Unsupported
Same EU format issues + year ambiguity
Compact Formats
yyyyMMdd
20240115
❌ Unsupported
No separators - not recognized


ddMMyyyy
15012024
❌ Unsupported
No separators - not recognized
Relative Dates
today, yesterday
today
❌ Unsupported
Not parsed


+7 days, -3 weeks
+7 days
❌ Unsupported
Not parsed
Unix Timestamps
Epoch seconds
1705305600
❌ Unsupported
Parsed as number, not date


Epoch milliseconds
1705305600000
❌ Unsupported
Parsed as number, not date
Other Languages
dd mois yyyy
15 janvier 2024
❌ Unsupported
Non-English month names


dd mes yyyy
15 enero 2024
❌ Unsupported
Spanish months
Weekday Formats
Day, MMM dd yyyy
Mon, Jan 15 2024
⚠️ Maybe
Weekday ignored if present, might work
Ambiguous Separators
yyyy MM dd
2024 01 15
⚠️ Maybe
Spaces as separators - unreliable



Format Status Legend

Symbol
Status
Meaning
✅ Tested
Guaranteed
Test cases exist in codebase, guaranteed to work
✅ Supported
Very Likely
No test cases but InvariantCulture supports it
⚠️ FAILS
Will Fail
Parse will throw error or return false
❌ WRONG
Data Corruption
Parses successfully but gives WRONG date
❌ Unsupported
Not Recognized
Format not supported by parser



Recommendations

✅Safe Formats(Use These)
    ISO 8601: 2024-01-15 (BEST CHOICE)
    Month names: Jan 15, 2024 or 15 Jan 2024
    ISO with time: 2024-01-15 10:30:00
❌Dangerous Formats(Avoid These)
    EU format: 15/01/2024 - Will fail or corrupt data
    Two-digit years: 01/15/24 - Ambiguous
    No separators: 20240115 - Not recognized
⚠️If You Must Use EU Format
You need to implement custom parsing logic (see my previous response with Option 1 or Option 2).
Would you like me to implement support for EU format, or help you standardize the date formats in your data sources?

New ETL - ClassPass - Custom events ingestion - AI SDLC
description:
ClassPass has custom events in S3 that needs to be ingested and used for data enrichment..

Even though we have S3 connector, this file is supposed to use redshift schema with the same CSV headers instead of our regular Cost and attribution headers.

This will take some effort and has to be done for GCS and GoogleDrive as well
